channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The U.S. will resume Ukraine military aid and intelligence, as Kyiv approves ceasefire
item.description: The United States said it will resume sending military aid and intelligence to Ukraine, as Ukraine agreed to a Trump administration proposal for a monthlong ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis, Michele Kelemen, and Eleanor Beardsley, MPR News

item.title: In his own words: Trump takes credit for stock market rises but casts aside blame for sell-off
item.description: President Donald Trump in recent days has dismissed fears of a recession and brushed aside the stock market sell-off, going so far as to say, “You can’t really watch the stock market.”.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration halts $1 billion program that keeps aging affordable housing livable
item.description: The Associated Press has obtained a document showing that the Trump administration is halting a $1 billion program that helps preserve affordable housing.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department is expected to slash public corruption unit, AP sources say
item.description: Prosecutors in the Justice Department section that handles public corruption cases have been told the unit will be significantly reduced in size, and its cases will be transferred to U.S. attorney’s offices around the country, according to two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: U of M says it's committed to combating antisemitism amid federal probes
item.description: The University of Minnesota says it’s working to address antisemitism on campus, amid federal investigations spurred by protests against Israel. Recent federal actions are raising alarms for pro-Palestinian and civil rights advocates, who say it’s a violation of free speech.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: US judge temporarily halts Trump plan to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston on Tuesday blocked the Trump administration's plan to cut hundreds of millions of dollars for teacher training, finding that cuts are already affecting training programs aimed at addressing a nationwide teacher shortage. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Trump California sheriff reacts to former Orange County Rep. Katie Porter's gubernatorial bid
item.description: Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, who joined the race to be California’s next governor in February, has issued a statement reacting to former U.S. Rep. and Senate candidate Katie Porter, who announced her intent to run for governor Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans warn Trump not to let trade war drag as he doubles down on tariff threats
item.description: Senate Republicans are warning President Trump to not let his trade war drag on as his latest escalation sent Wall Street further into turmoil.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks Michael George DeSombre to represent US in East Asia and Pacific
item.description: President Donald Trump announced his nomination of Michael George DeSombre, who was nominated to serve as the next Assistant Secretary of State for East Asia and the Pacific.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom's viral 'Latinx' claim crumbles amid scrutiny of his own administration's online records
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom falsely claimed that his office has never used the term Latinx, despite various posts, web pages and documents analyzed by Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump floats labeling violence against Tesla dealerships as domestic terrorism
item.description: President Trump hinted he would be open to labeling individuals who carry out violence at Tesla dealerships as “domestic terrorists” following a string of violent demonstrations at the electric vehicle company’s showrooms across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Law firm sues over Trump executive order that seeks to suspend security clearances
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A law firm targeted by President Donald Trump over its legal services during the 2016 presidential campaign sued the federal government Tuesday over an executive order that seeks to strip its attorneys of security clearances.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada suspends 25% electricity tariffs on US
item.description: This comes after what Ford said was a "productive" conversation with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Vance gives House GOP closing pitch on Trump-backed funding bill ahead of vote
item.description: Vice President Vance delivered a closing pitch in favor of Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) government funding bill during the House GOP’s private conference meeting Tuesday, hours before the lower chamber is scheduled to vote on the measure.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: How reliable is the government’s economic data? Under Trump, there are real concerns
item.description: The disbanding of committees that consulted on government data — and comments from a senior official about changing how GDP is calculated — are raising alarm about the reliability of government data.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: US, Ukraine talk peace as Kyiv, Moscow trade drone attacks
item.description: Officials from Ukraine and the United States have gathered in Saudi Arabia to discuss a potential limited ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky bourbon makers fear becoming 'collateral damage' in Trump's trade war
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — With a new distillery set to open soon, the makers of Brough Brothers bourbon in Kentucky were ready to put their business plan into action. They were looking to ramp up whiskey production to break into lucrative new markets in Canada and Europe.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's conservatives trying to associate Carney with Trudeau
item.description: Conservatives are on the defense after the Liberal Party picked Carney as Canada's next leader, a journalist says.
item.dc:creator: Connell McShane, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Lori Chavez-DeRemer as Labor secretary
item.description: The Senate on Monday confirmed former Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer (R-Ore.) to lead the Department of Labor, cementing another of President Trump’s nominees in bipartisan fashion. Senators voted 67 to 32, with 17 Democrats voted with most Republicans present. Sens. Rand Paul (R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Immigration needs to be 'shut down' until unity restored: Carlson
item.description: President Donald Trump has pledged history's largest domestic deportation, but some fear how long the process could take
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Karl Rove: Trump claims of DOGE 'fraud' discoveries will backfire
item.description: Republican strategist Karl Rove agreed with James Carville, who had urged Democrats to make a "strategic political retreat," advising them "to play dead.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, NewsNation

item.title: US added to human rights watchlist over Trump moves
item.description: The United States has been added to CIVICUS's monitor list of potential human rights threats due to President Trump's restrictive executive orders and institutional cutbacks, which are creating an atmosphere to chill democratic dissent.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Mark Carney, elected next Canadian prime minister
item.description: Mark Carney, a former central banker, won his Liberal Party's election in a landslide on Sunday to replace Justin Trudeau as prime minister of Canada. He will be sworn into office in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Tampa Bay Ukrainian refugees worried temporary legal status could be revoked
item.description: It has been three years since Russia invaded Ukraine sparking the latest deadly conflict between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats urge Lombardo to hire fired federal workers
item.description: Democratic leaders in the Nevada Assembly are urging Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo to hire federal workers laid off as part of recent firings from President Donald Trump and Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: More states want to stop taxing groceries
item.description: The number of states imposing sales taxes on groceries has shrunk over the years, and the number may decrease further in the coming months
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
